name: MCP Gateway
what_it_does: A plugin-based MCP gateway that sits between an LLM and other MCP servers, intercepting and sanitizing requests/responses to prevent PII leakage, token/secret exposure, prompt injection attacks, and harmful content — with built-in security scanning of MCP server reputation.
use_cases: Adding a security and PII protection layer in front of existing MCP servers without modifying them
Detecting and blocking prompt injection attempts embedded in MCP tool responses before they reach the LLM
Auditing and logging MCP tool calls for compliance and observability via the xetrack plugin
not_for: Simple personal MCP setups where security overhead is unnecessary
Teams that need full enterprise policy management without Lasso Security's commercial API key (basic/presidio plugins have limited coverage)
High-throughput production environments where the Python proxy layer adds latency concerns
best_when: You are deploying MCP servers in an enterprise context and need a drop-in security layer for PII masking, secret detection, and prompt injection defense without rewriting existing MCP servers.
avoid_when: You want full security coverage (PII + secrets + prompt injection + harmful content) without the Lasso commercial plugin — the open-source plugins only partially cover these cases.

auth.notes: Lasso plugin requires LASSO_API_KEY. The gateway itself has no auth — it relies on the underlying MCP client's auth. xetrack uses path-based config.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed core; Lasso plugin requires commercial API key for full security coverage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Sanitization errors may be opaque to the agent — the gateway may silently redact content without clear explanation of what was removed.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Gateway is a passthrough proxy — idempotency matches the underlying MCP servers.
known_agent_gotchas: Silent redaction of PII/secrets means agents may receive incomplete tool responses without understanding why
Full security coverage (prompt injection + harmful content) requires the commercial Lasso plugin
Security scanner checks reputation at load time but does not re-scan at runtime if a server is compromised later
Python proxy adds latency hop — not benchmarked or documented
